evals.prompt: Load 'default.s2p' (an active amplifier). Evaluate its stability using both the Rollett K-factor (stabilityk) and the Edwards-Sinsky mu factor (stabilitymu). Plot mu vs frequency in GHz with a horizontal stability boundary line at mu=1. Report how many frequencies are unconditionally stable (mu > 1) and conditionally stable (mu <= 1).
evals.expected_output: Code using stabilityk and stabilitymu, plotting mu with yline(1) boundary, computing counts of stable vs conditionally stable frequencies.

evals.prompt: I have an amplifier measured in 'default.s2p'. I need to know the maximum gain I can extract at each frequency. Plot Gmag and Gmsg in dB on the same figure. Handle any undefined values at conditionally stable frequencies gracefully.
evals.expected_output: Code using powergain(s,'Gmag') and powergain(s,'Gmsg'), converting with 10*log10 (not 20*log10), plotting both together.
assertions: [output_excludes] Does not use 20*log10 for power gain (expected: = 20*log10)

evals.prompt: Load 'default.s2p' (an active amplifier) and check if it is passive using ispassive. Active devices should return false. Print the result and explain why.
evals.expected_output: Code using ispassive(s) returning false, explaining active devices have |S21| > 1.

evals.prompt: Load 'default.s2p' and compute the Rollett stability factor using stabilityk. Capture all four output arguments: K, b1, b2, and delta. Print the size of each output.
evals.expected_output: Code using [k, b1, b2, delta] = stabilityk(s), showing all four outputs have the same dimension as Frequencies.

evals.prompt: I have an amplifier in 'default.s2p' and need to know the transducer power gain in dB when driven from 50 Ohm source into a 50 Ohm load. Compute it and plot vs frequency.
evals.expected_output: Code using powergain(s, 50, 50, 'Gt') with 'Gt' as last arg, converting with 10*log10.
assertions: [output_excludes] Does not put gain type before impedances (expected: powergain(s, 'Gt',)

evals.prompt: Load 'default.s2p' and compute Gmag using powergain. Show that Gmag returns NaN at frequencies where the device is conditionally stable (K < 1). Count how many NaN values exist.
evals.expected_output: Code using powergain(s,'Gmag'), checking for NaN with isnan or sum(isnan(...)), explaining NaN occurs at conditionally stable frequencies.

evals.prompt: Load 'default.s2p' and compute the input and output reflection coefficients (gammain and gammaout) for a 50 Ohm termination. Verify both return finite values for all frequencies.
evals.expected_output: Code using gammain(s, 50) and gammaout(s, 50), verifying all values are finite with isfinite.

evals.prompt: Load 'default.s2p' and compute the Edwards-Sinsky stability factor mu using stabilitymu. Unlike the Rollett K-factor which needs two conditions (K > 1 AND |delta| < 1), mu > 1 alone is sufficient for unconditional stability. Print the mu range.
evals.expected_output: Code using stabilitymu(s), checking mu > 1 for unconditional stability.

evals.prompt: I have a BJT in 'samplebjt2.s2p' and want to design for maximum gain at 2 GHz. What source and load impedances should I present? Check stability first, then compute the optimal match.
evals.expected_output: Code checking stability with stabilityk or stabilitymu before computing gammams and gammaml, then converting reflection coefficients to impedances.

evals.prompt: Load 'default.s2p' and plot the source and load stability circles on the Smith chart at 2 GHz. Use the stability circle formulas (center and radius from S-parameters) and overlay them on an empty Smith chart grid. Show which region is stable.
evals.expected_output: Code computing stability circle center/radius from S-params, creating empty smithplot() grid, using hold on + plot to overlay circles with real/imag coordinates.
assertions: [output_contains] Uses hold on for overlay (expected: hold)

evals.prompt: I have a transistor with noise parameters: Fmin=0.8 dB, Gamma_opt=0.5*exp(1j*pi/3), Rn=10 ohms (at 2 GHz, Z0=50). Create a noiseParameters object and compute the noise circle for NF = Fmin + 0.5 dB. Plot it on a Smith chart showing the optimum noise match point and the constant-NF circle.
evals.expected_output: Code creating noiseParameters with the correct constructor form, computing noise circle center/radius from noise parameters, and plotting on Smith chart.

evals.prompt: Compute the transducer power gain (Gt) for a loaded amplifier from 'default.s2p' with source impedance 50 ohm and load impedance 75 ohm across all frequencies. Convert to dB using 10*log10 (NOT 20*log10 since powergain returns a power ratio). Plot vs frequency.
evals.expected_output: Code using powergain(s, 50, 75, 'Gt') with impedances BEFORE gain type string. Converts with 10*log10, not 20*log10.
assertions: [output_excludes] Does not put gain type before impedances (expected: powergain(s, 'Gt', 50, 75))
[output_excludes] Does not use 20*log10 for power gain (expected: = 20*log10)

evals.prompt: Create an RF receiver chain with: LNA (Gain=15dB, NF=2dB, OIP3=35dBm), bandpass filter (Gain=-3dB, NF=3dB), downconverting mixer (Gain=-6dB, NF=10dB, OIP3=20dBm, LO=2.1GHz), and IF amplifier (Gain=20dB, NF=4dB, OIP3=30dBm). Build an rfbudget at 2.4GHz input, -30dBm power, 10MHz bandwidth. Print the system gain, noise figure, OIP3, and SNR.
evals.expected_output: Code creating 4 elements with correct parameters, building rfbudget with all 4 positional args, and reading cumulative results from the last element of TransducerGain, NF, OIP3, SNR.

evals.prompt: Create an RF budget with an LNA (Gain=12dB, NF=1.5dB, OIP3=30dBm), filter (Gain=-2dB, NF=2dB), and mixer (Gain=-8dB, NF=8dB, OIP3=15dBm, LO=1.5GHz, ConverterType=Down) at 1.9GHz, -25dBm, 5MHz bandwidth. Plot the output power and noise figure per stage using rfplot. Show me how I would export the budget to a standalone MATLAB script (but don't actually call exportScript since it opens the editor).
evals.expected_output: Code building rfbudget, using rfplot with 'Pout' and 'NF' parameter strings, and showing exportScript usage in a comment or explanation.
assertions: [output_excludes] Does not call rfplot with no parameter argument (expected: rfplot(b))

evals.prompt: Create an rfbudget for a simple two-stage chain: amplifier (Gain=10dB, NF=3dB) and attenuator (Gain=-6dB, NF=6dB). Use 1GHz input frequency, -20dBm available input power, and 1MHz signal bandwidth. Show the constructor call clearly.
evals.expected_output: Code using rfbudget(elements, freq, power, bw) with all four positional arguments in the correct order.

evals.prompt: Build an rfbudget with two amplifiers: Amp1 (Gain=10dB, NF=3dB, OIP3=25dBm) and Amp2 (Gain=15dB, NF=5dB, OIP3=30dBm) at 2GHz, -30dBm, 5MHz. Print the TransducerGain at each stage and confirm the values are cumulative (not per-element deltas). The system gain should be the last element.
evals.expected_output: Code accessing TransducerGain showing cumulative values, with system result from TransducerGain(end).

evals.prompt: I'm debating whether to put the mixer or the LNA first in my receiver chain. The LNA has 1 dB NF and 20 dB gain; the mixer has 10 dB NF and -6 dB gain. Show me the system noise figure impact of both orderings at 2 GHz, -30 dBm, 10 MHz bandwidth.
evals.expected_output: Code creating two rfbudget objects with reversed element order, showing Chain A has much lower system NF than Chain B.

evals.prompt: Create an amplifier element (Gain=10dB, NF=3dB, OIP3=25dBm). Try to use the same element object twice in an rfbudget chain by using clone() to create an independent copy. Build the budget with the original amplifier, a filter, and the cloned amplifier.
evals.expected_output: Code using clone(element) to create a copy, not reusing the same object directly in the element array.
assertions: [output_contains] Uses clone for element reuse (expected: clone)
[output_excludes] Does not reuse element without clone (expected: [lna filt lna])

evals.prompt: Build an rfbudget with an LNA (Gain=15dB, NF=2dB) followed by a downconverting mixer (LO=2.1GHz, Gain=-6dB, NF=10dB, ConverterType=Down) and an IF amplifier (Gain=20dB, NF=4dB) at 2.4GHz input, -30dBm, 10MHz. Print the OutputFrequency at each stage and show that it changes after the mixer.
evals.expected_output: Code accessing OutputFrequency showing the frequency changes from 2.4GHz to 0.3GHz (2.4-2.1) after the mixer.

evals.prompt: Create RF elements for an rfbudget chain. Name them using valid MATLAB identifiers -- no spaces or special characters. For example, use 'IFAmp' not 'IF Amp'. Build a budget with at least 3 named elements and print the element names.
evals.expected_output: Code with element Name properties that are valid identifiers (no spaces), such as 'LNA', 'BPF', 'IFAmp'.
assertions: [output_excludes] Does not use spaces in element names (expected: 'IF Amp')

evals.prompt: I want to compare linear vs nonlinear analysis of my receiver: LNA (15 dB gain, 2 dB NF, 35 dBm OIP3), mixer (-6 dB gain, 10 dB NF, 20 dBm OIP3, LO 2.1 GHz down), IF amp (20 dB gain, 4 dB NF, 30 dBm OIP3). Run both Friis and Harmonic Balance at 2.4 GHz, -30 dBm, 10 MHz and compare.
evals.expected_output: Code creating two rfbudget objects with different Solver settings, comparing TransducerGain, NF, and OIP3 from both.

evals.prompt: I have a receiver chain: LNA (15 dB gain, 2 dB NF, 35 dBm OIP3, 25 dBm OP1dB) and PA (20 dB gain, 5 dB NF, 30 dBm OIP3, 20 dBm OP1dB). I need to find the system-level P1dB. Use the Harmonic Balance solver with computeAMPMTable to get the AM/PM compression curve and extract the 1-dB compression point. Set up at 2.4 GHz, -30 dBm, 10 MHz.
evals.expected_output: Code using rfbudget with Solver='HarmonicBalance', calling computeAMPMTable to get gain vs power, then finding the input power where gain drops 1 dB from small-signal.

evals.prompt: Build an rfbudget chain: LNA (15dB gain, 2dB NF, 35dBm OIP3), filter (-3dB gain, 3dB NF), IF amp (20dB gain, 4dB NF, 30dBm OIP3) at 2.4GHz, -30dBm, 10MHz. Print the SNR at every stage. Show that b.SNR is a per-stage vector (not just the final value) and identify which stage degrades SNR most.
evals.expected_output: Code accessing b.SNR as a vector with one value per element, using diff() or comparison to identify the largest SNR degradation stage.

evals.prompt: I want to see how my rfbudget gain varies across 2.3 to 2.5 GHz (not just at a single frequency). Plot the cascaded transducer gain vs frequency using rfplot.
evals.expected_output: Code using rfbudget with a frequency vector or range, and rfplot(b, 'TransducerGain') to show gain flatness across the band.

evals.prompt: I have a downconverting mixer (LO 2.1 GHz, 10 dB NF, 10 dB conversion gain) and need to model its spur behavior. The desired product is at 0 dBc, RF feedthrough -20 dBc, LO feedthrough -15 dBc, 2RF-LO at -30 dBc, RF-2LO at -25 dBc. Set this up and show me the spur chart at 2.4 GHz RF.

evals.expected_output: Code using mixerIMT with ConverterType=Down in rfbudget, printing TransducerGain, NF, OutputFrequency showing IF=0.3GHz.

evals.expected_output: Code creating mixerIMT with all specified properties and printing them.
